title: tape wiring: input ring retention, output/log/error regeneration
manager: The full tape wiring — a bounded input ring that retains recent inputs, and regenerated output, log, and error tapes — so a running machine has a complete, replayable record of what came in and what went out. Tapes are the substrate for replay, diagnostics, and the later flight recorder; wiring them properly here is what makes a run a reproducible artifact you can hand to someone else rather than a transient event that's gone the moment it finishes.
cs: input ring retention + output/log/error tape regeneration (fsl#450/#457/#913/#912); the tape family behind replay and the era-4 flight recorder; commit-time recording.
